policy_text: Vodafone connects over 275 million customers across 15 countries through mobile and fixed services.
Vodafone serves an additional 66 million customers through joint ventures and associates.

Vodafone's portfolio includes fixed-line broadband, television services, Internet of Things (IoT) connectivity with over 205 million connections, and financial technology (FinTech) services reaching 88 million users globally.
Vodafone Business serves 4.7 million enterprise customers, representing 26% of group service revenue and addressing a €144 billion addressable market.
Vodafone invests in cutting‑edge technologies including 5G, artificial intelligence, and IoT to provide faster, more reliable, and personalized services.
Vodafone develops next‑generation networks with significant investments in 5G infrastructure across key markets.
Vodafone creates an inclusive digital ecosystem that bridges the digital divide, particularly in underserved rural communities.
Vodafone provides financial inclusion through mobile payment solutions, notably M‑Pesa.
Vodafone offers cloud computing, IoT platforms, cybersecurity, and unified communications services.
Vodafone operates owned networks in 15 countries and partner networks in 46 countries.
Vodafone Global Enterprise serves multinational corporate clients across 65 countries.
Vodafone provides mobile telecommunications services (voice, data, messaging).
Vodafone provides fixed‑line broadband and television services.
Vodafone aims to achieve net‑zero carbon emissions by 2040.
Vodafone commits to environmental sustainability through renewable energy adoption and carbon‑emission reduction initiatives.
Vodafone’s mission is to become a new‑generation connectivity and digital services provider for Europe and Africa, enabling an inclusive and sustainable digital society.
Vodafone’s vision includes driving digital inclusion and sustainable practices, making technology accessible to underserved communities.
Vodafone strives to deliver superior customer experiences through technological innovation.
Net‑zero emissions must be achieved no later than the year 2040.
All prices are subject to annual inflationary adjustments and applicable taxes.
Terms and conditions apply to all products and services listed.
EU roaming (fair use policy applies).
Minimum contract term: 12 or 24 months.
Data rollover (up to next month, selected plans).
5G network access at no additional charge.
VeryMe Rewards loyalty program access.
Entertainment add-on options (Amazon Prime Video, Spotify Premium).
Flexible prepaid plans with no long-term commitments.
Unlimited UK calls and texts.
Unlimited standard SMS and MMS messages within UK.
Unlimited minutes to UK standard mobiles and landlines (01, 02, 03).
Unlimited Lite plan price will increase to £17.44 in April 2026.
Unlimited plan price will increase to £21.80 in April 2026.
Unlimited Max plan price will increase to £28.34 in April 2026.
Unlimited Plus Xtra plan price will increase to £24.99 in April 2027.
Pay As You Go Big Value Bundles are offered.
source_section: Pay As You Go Big Value Bundles

policy_text: Vodafone provides FinTech & Mobile Financial Services.
Vodafone provides Enterprise Digital Services (Cloud, Security, Unified Communications).
Vodafone provides Internet of Things (IoT) Connectivity & Platforms.
Vodafone provides 5G Ultra network capability.
Vodafone leverages AI-powered solutions.
Confidential — Internal Use Only: The document must not be disclosed outside Vodafone India or its authorized personnel.
The Marketing & Brand Guide is intended solely for internal teams involved in marketing, communications, product development, and commercial strategy.
Vodafone Idea Limited must trade commercially under the unified brand name 'Vi'.
5G coverage must reach at least 50 cities by the end of FY25 for major operators.
Vi utilizes the following spectrum bands: Sub‑GHz (700 MHz), mid‑band (3.5 GHz), and mmWave (26 GHz).
Vi offers enterprise 5G services including network slicing and private 5G.
Vi's average revenue per user (ARPU) target for FY24‑25 is INR 145 with a minimum year‑over‑year growth of 10%.
Vi must comply with the Telecom Act 2023, all DoT and TRAI interconnection regulations, and data localization requirements.
All customer data must be stored within India in accordance with data localization requirements.
Vi's brand identity guidelines require all marketing assets to reflect the values of togetherness, optimism, and digital empowerment.
Vi's partnerships with OTT platforms (Netflix, Amazon Prime, SonyLIV) are to be leveraged to provide digital lifestyle subscriptions via SIM cards.

Make technology accessible to underserved communities, driving digital inclusion and sustainable practices.

Connect people and businesses globally through seamless connectivity solutions.

Total Customers (Including JVs): 341 million

Countries with Partner Networks: 46

Business Customers: 4.7 million
IoT Connections: 205 million

Capital Expenditure (2024): €36.7 billion
FTSE 100 constituent
27th global market cap ranking
Environmental sustainability through renewable energy adoption and carbon emission reduction initiatives
Innovation in digital services including cloud computing, IoT platforms, cybersecurity, and unified communications

Primary Industry: Telecommunications Services
Mobile telecommunications (voice, data, messaging)
Fixed-line broadband and television services
Internet of Things (IoT) connectivity and platforms
Cloud computing and enterprise digital services
Financial technology (mobile payments and banking)
Cybersecurity and unified communications
Primary Listing: London Stock Exchange (FTSE 100 Index)
Secondary Listing: NASDAQ (American Depositary Receipts)
Vodafone Business serves 4.7 million business customers globally with cloud, IoT, security, unified communications, and managed services.
Vodafone Consumer Services provides mobile and fixed services for residential customers across Europe and Africa, including voice, data, broadband, and television.
Vodafone FinTech Services offers mobile financial services, including the M-Pesa platform.
Acquisition of Cable & Wireless Worldwide provides Vodafone access to fixed-line network infrastructure.
Acquisition of Bluefish Communications formed the nucleus of Vodafone's Unified Communications and Collaboration practice.
Vodafone has made massive investments in 4G/LTE network rollout across European markets.
Vodafone launched and expanded the M-Pesa mobile money platform, transforming financial inclusion in Africa.
Vodafone Global Enterprise serves multinational corporate clients across 65 countries.
Vodafone expanded its presence across Africa and Asia-Pacific regions.
Strategic acquisitions in Turkey (Telsim) and a majority stake in Hutchison Essar (India) extend Vodafone's market footprint.
Merger with AirTouch Communications created a transatlantic telecommunications powerhouse.
Acquisition of Mannesmann AG for £112 billion was the largest corporate takeover in history, expanding Vodafone's global scale.
Vodafone serves 88 million users with mobile money, payments, and banking solutions.
Vodafone Global Enterprise provides specialized services to multinational corporations including Deutsche Post, The Linde Group, Unilever, and Volkswagen Group.
Vodafone maintains direct network ownership and operations in 15 markets across Europe and Africa.
Vodafone has been executing 5G and Digital Transformation initiatives since 2020.
Vodafone commits to invest €8 billion annually in capital expenditure, primarily for 5G network deployment.
Vodafone maintains strategic partnerships with Microsoft, Google, and Accenture to deliver cloud and AI‑powered services.
Vodafone merged Vodafone UK with Three UK, approved December 2024 and completed May 2025.
Vodafone expanded IoT services to 205 million connections for enterprise and industrial customers.
Vodafone developed FinTech services that reach 88 million users globally.
Vodafone invested £200 million in digital skills programs in 2024.
Vodafone operates joint ventures and associate partnerships in 5 markets, serving 66 million customers.
Vodafone leverages partner networks in 46 markets to provide roaming and service partnerships extending global reach.
Vodafone’s core value of 5G Network Leadership mandates accelerated deployment of 5G.
Achieve net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.
source_section: Sustainability – Environmental commitment with net-zero emissions target by 2040

policy_text: Reduce carbon emissions by 30% by 2024 compared to the 2020 baseline.
source_section: Sustainability – Environmental commitment with net-zero emissions target by 2040

policy_text: Connect 2 million rural households to high-speed internet in FY 2024 as part of digital inclusion initiatives.
source_section: Inclusion – Digital inclusion initiatives connecting 2 million rural households to high-speed internet in FY 2024

policy_text: Handle 50 million customer queries in 2024 using AI-powered chatbots.
source_section: Customer Experience Enhancement – Deployment of AI-powered customer service solutions (50 million queries handled by AI chatbots in 2024)

policy_text: Invest £1.5 billion in research and development during FY 2024.
source_section: Innovation – Continuous investment in research and development (£1.5 billion in FY 2024)

policy_text: Deliver superior customer experiences through reliable network services, cutting-edge technology, and responsive support.
source_section: Customer Centricity – Dedication to delivering superior customer experiences through reliable network services, cutting-edge technology, and responsive support

policy_text: Maintain ethical business practices and transparent governance aligned with international standards and regulatory requirements.
source_section: Integrity – Ethical business practices and transparent governance aligned with international standards and regulatory requirements

policy_text: Develop platforms, cybersecurity, and AI-powered solutions as strategic priorities for 2025-2027.
Grow the FinTech user base to exceed 88 million, with a focus on African markets.
source_section: Financial Technology Expansion – Growth of FinTech user base beyond 88 million, particularly in African markets where mobile money transforms financial access

policy_text: Optimize portfolio, streamline assets, and standardize operations to improve financial performance and shareholder value.
source_section: Operational Efficiency – Portfolio optimization, asset streamlining, and operational standardization to improve financial performance and shareholder value

policy_text: Progress toward net-zero emissions by 2040 through renewable energy investments and energy-efficiency measures.
source_section: Sustainability Leadership – Continued progress toward net-zero emissions by 2040 through renewable energy investments and energy efficiency measures

policy_text: Deploy AI-powered customer service solutions to improve response times and satisfaction, handling 50 million queries in 2024.
source_section: Customer Experience Enhancement – Deployment of AI-powered customer service solutions (50 million queries handled by AI chatbots in 2024) to improve response times and satisfaction

policy_text: Achieve 26.1% growth over the last two years in B2B digital services, including cloud computing and IoT.
source_section: Digital Services Growth – Expansion of B2B digital services (26.1% growth over last 2 years) including cloud computing, IoT

policy_text: Create a connected digital society where data flows rapidly, linking people, communities, and devices via 5G and IoT innovations.
source_section: Digital Society – Commitment to creating a connected digital society where data flows rapidly, connecting people, communities, and devices through innovations like 5G and IoT

Unlimited Lite plan price increases to £17.44 in April 2026.
Vodafone serves over 275 million customers across 15 markets with owned operations, plus an additional 66 million through joint ventures and associates.
Our product portfolio leverages cutting‑edge technologies including 5G Ultra, IoT connectivity, cloud computing, and AI‑powered solutions.

Product categories include Consumer Mobile Services, Business Mobile Solutions, Fixed Broadband & Home Services, Internet of Things (IoT) Connectivity & Platforms, Enterprise Digital Services, FinTech & Mobile Financial Services.

policy_text: Standard plan price will increase to £21.80 in April 2026.
Maximum monthly charge for the Standard unlimited plan is £26.
Maximum monthly charge for maximum available speeds will increase to £28.34 in April 2026.
Standard plan provides 15GB of data at £14 per month.

Plus plan provides 30GB of data at £18 per month.
Plus plan contract term can be 12 or 24 months.
Premium plan provides 100GB of data at £24 per month.
Premium plan contract term can be 12 or 24 months.
All plans include unlimited UK calls and texts.

All plans include EU roaming subject to a fair use policy.
All plans include VeryMe Rewards membership.

All plans provide unlimited minutes to UK standard mobiles and landlines (01, 02, 03).
All plans provide unlimited standard SMS and MMS messages within the UK.
Entertainment add-on options such as Amazon Prime Video and Spotify Premium are available.

Flexible prepaid plans are offered with no long-term commitments.
7GB Bundle is priced at £10 and includes unlimited UK calls & texts.
15GB Bundle is priced at £15 and includes unlimited UK calls & texts and Zone A roaming.
40GB Bundle is priced at £20 and includes unlimited UK calls & texts and Zone A roaming.
Zone A roaming is included in the 15GB and 40GB bundles.
Data rollover is limited to the next month and only for selected plans.
100GB Bundle priced at £25 includes 100GB data, unlimited UK calls & texts, and Zone A roaming.
Unlimited Bundle priced at £30 includes unlimited calls, texts, data, and Zone A roaming.
Standard PAYG call rate is 35p per minute.
Standard PAYG text rate is 10p per message.
Standard PAYG data rate is £1 per day for 500MB.
Bundles automatically renew every 30 days if sufficient credit is available.
Customers may opt out of auto‑renewal at any time by calling 2345.
SIM Only 10GB plan costs £8 per month.
SIM Only 10GB plan provides 10GB of data.
SIM Only 10GB plan operates on a 30‑day rolling contract term.
SIM Only 20GB plan costs £12 per month.
SIM Only 20GB plan provides 20GB of data.
SIM Only 20GB plan operates on a 30‑day rolling contract term.
SIM Only 50GB plan costs £16 per month.
SIM Only 50GB plan provides 50GB of data.
SIM Only 50GB plan has a 12‑month contract term.
All bundles include Zone A roaming.
Big Value Bundles are 30‑day bundles unless a different contract term is specified.
No upfront costs are charged for any SIM Only plan.
Contracts may only be either 30‑day or 12‑month flexible terms.
Customers are allowed to keep their existing phone number when switching plans.
Full 5G network access is included with all plans.
EU roaming is included with all plans.
The SIM Only 100GB plan is priced at £20 per month for a 12‑month term and provides 100 GB of data.
The SIM Only Unlimited plan is priced at £25 per month for a 12‑month term and provides unlimited data.
iPhone 16 Pro financing requires an upfront payment of £99 and £42 per month for 24 months.
Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra financing requires an upfront payment of £79 and £38 per month for 24 months.
Google Pixel 10 Pro financing requires an upfront payment of £49 and £32 per month for 24 months.
Samsung Galaxy A16 financing requires no upfront payment and £15 per month for 24 months.
HONOR Magic V5 (Foldable) financing requires an upfront payment of £149 and £45 per month for 36 months.
Device financing and handset offers are available for popular smartphones.
Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra includes flagship performance, an advanced camera system, and an S Pen.
